scanner_note: Prototype data only. The UI has not run local OS checks.

findings.title: Firewall is off for public networks
findings.detail: The sample result shows the public firewall profile disabled.
findings.why_it_matters: On public networks, an inactive firewall can expose services that should stay blocked.
fix_steps: Open Windows Security.
Go to Firewall and network protection.
Turn the firewall on for public networks.

findings.title: Remote Desktop appears exposed
findings.detail: The sample result shows TCP port 3389 listening on all local interfaces.
findings.why_it_matters: Remote Desktop should not be reachable unless it is required and protected.
fix_steps: Turn off Remote Desktop if you do not use it.
If you need it, restrict it to trusted networks.
Use a strong password and keep Windows updated.

findings.title: Security signatures are out of date
findings.detail: The sample result shows antivirus signatures last updated 11 days ago.
findings.why_it_matters: Old signatures can miss recent malware and suspicious files.
fix_steps: Open Windows Security.
Go to Virus and threat protection.
Run a protection update.

findings.title: Password policy is weak
findings.detail: The sample result shows no maximum password age configured.
findings.why_it_matters: A weak password policy makes stale or reused passwords more likely.
fix_steps: Review local password rules.
Use long passwords or passphrases.
Enable multi factor authentication where available.

findings.title: System updates need attention
findings.detail: The sample result shows the last successful update was 32 days ago.
findings.why_it_matters: Security updates close known vulnerabilities attackers often reuse.
fix_steps: Open Settings.
Go to Windows Update.
Install pending security updates and restart if needed.

findings.title: Guest account is disabled
findings.detail: The sample result shows no active Guest account.
findings.why_it_matters: Disabled guest access reduces casual access to the device.
fix_steps: No action needed.

findings.title: User Account Control is enabled
findings.detail: The sample result shows UAC enabled.
findings.why_it_matters: UAC helps stop unexpected changes from running with administrator rights.
fix_steps: Keep UAC enabled.

findings.title: Common web ports are closed
findings.detail: The sample result shows ports 80 and 443 are not listening locally.
findings.why_it_matters: Closed unused ports reduce the machine surface visible on the network.
fix_steps: No action needed.

findings.title: Disk encryption is detected
findings.detail: The sample result shows device encryption enabled.
findings.why_it_matters: Disk encryption helps protect data if the device is lost or stolen.
fix_steps: Keep your recovery key stored safely.

findings.title: No suspicious TEMP startup entry
findings.detail: The sample result shows no startup entry running from a temporary folder.
findings.why_it_matters: Malware often persists through startup entries in unusual locations.
fix_steps: No action needed.

findings.title: Some checks may need administrator access
findings.detail: The sample result includes checks that would need elevated permissions in a real scan.
findings.why_it_matters: Limited permissions can hide some local account or policy details.
fix_steps: Run the final packaged scanner as administrator only if you trust it and need full local detail.

findings.title: Enterprise update history was not available
findings.detail: The sample result marks this check as unsupported for the current prototype data.
findings.why_it_matters: Skipped checks are shown for transparency but do not change the score.
fix_steps: No action needed in this UI prototype.
